ead.archdesc:
======= RMM04378Helen Vandervort papers4378Compiled by Carolyn SmithDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Feb. 26, 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
socsc humecThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:49:53 UTC.12/07/2018

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Helen Vandervort papers
Vandervort, Helen, 1905-1994.
Randles, Eleanor Foster, 1920-
Babbitt, Kathleen
4378
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet.
1922-1994. Correspondence, financial records, reports, speeches, photographs, printed materials Correspondence, financial records, reports, speeches, photographs, printed materials, certificates, and biography relating to Vandervort's work with the Tompkins County Public Library and the Finger Lakes Library System; the Tompkins County Senior Citizens' Council and senior citizens issues; the New York State College of Human Ecology; the New York State Fair; and women's rights issues. Also, letters from the Bull family (1922-26), interviews with Helen Vandervort by Eleanor Randles and Kathleen Babbitt. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 65 - folder 1 ======= 65 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2088107
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Carolyn Smith
Date completed:
February 2003
EAD encoding:
Carolyn Smith
Date modified:
RMC staff, January 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Extension education administrator.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Libraries and Miscellaneous
Box 1 Folders 1-42Box 2 Folders 1-15
Series II. Senior Citizens
Box 2 Folders 18-29Box 3 Folders 1-3Box 4 Folders 1-2
Series III. Printed Material
Box 4
Series IV. Biographical Information
Box 5Box 6 Folders 1-6
Series V. Correspondence
Box 6 Folders 7-41
Series VI. New York State Fair
Box 6 Folders 42-45
Series VII. Subject Files
Box 5 Folders 1-4Box 7 Folders 1-43
Cite As:
Helen Vandervort papers, #4378. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Correspondence, financial records, reports, speeches, photographs, printed material, certificates, and biographical material relating to Helen Vandervort's work with the Tompkins County Public Library and the Finger Lakes Library System; the Tompkins County Senior Citizens' Council and senior citizens issues; the New York State College of Human Ecology; the New York State Fair; Alpha House in Trumansburg, New York and women's rights issues. Also, letters from the Bull family (1922-26), and interviews with Helen Vandervort by Eleanor Randles and Kathleen Babbitt.
Photographs. Women -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Home economics extension workers. Home economics extension work. Bull family. Finger Lakes Library System Tompkins County Public Library Alpha House (Trumansburg, N.Y.) Tompkins County Senior Citizens\' Council New York State College of Human Ecology Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. New York State Fair
